summary: Read-only systems architecture review ACCEPTS the M7 strategic terrain recovery planning package for independent QA review. ADR-011 treats the current polygon-first player-facing map and spatial-index direction as superseded, not as a visual-polish target. The downstream schema, fixture/content, renderer-interaction, and validation tasks remain DRAFT and must not start from this review alone. This ACCEPT does not approve M7 content lock and does not start M7 exit validation.

decisions.decision: Accept the recovery planning package for independent qa_reviewer review only. This does not approve content lock, does not mark M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 accepted, and does not start downstream implementation.

ADR-011 explicitly says the latest product-owner clarification rejects the current drawing and spatial-index direction at root level, rejects visual polish/texture replacement/renderer-only terrain art, and supersedes the polygon-first player-facing map direction.

TerrainPatch, BarrierChannel, StrategicNode, RouteCorridor, and DistrictGovernanceFootprint are separated by responsibility: terrain readability, movement constraints/enablers, action anchors, explicit corridor authority, and governance overlay respectively.

The proposed chain correctly separates schema/query/save authority, COMPOSITE fixture/content, renderer-interaction replacement, and validation. Dependencies are sequential and status remains DRAFT for all downstream tasks.

ADR/task criteria prohibit hidden grid/lattice/hex, sequential-id order, centroid or bbox adjacency, geometry-only route inference, renderer-only fake routes, and governance adjacency as reachability/cost authority.

The plan requires explicit schema, save/content hash behavior, structured rejection or migration, deterministic tie-breaks, same query model for AI and player explanations, and hit-test priority from actionable nodes/corridors/blockers before governance overlays.

Allowed/forbidden paths are planning-bounded for recovery and explicitly do not authorize high-fidelity final art, formal GIS historical data, production cultural assets, server/multiplayer, telemetry/accounts/cloud saves, Rust/WASM, release/commercial/branding scope, or manual node battle reversal.

The downstream required_tests are adequate planning gates: they include package-specific tests, pnpm check, content validation, deterministic query/hash/save tests, anti-grid/lattice negative cases, hit-test priority, query parity, screenshots, E2E, and manual-smoke/console-clean evidence.

risks.summary: The requested short commands taskctl and validate-handoff are not available in this PowerShell PATH.
risks.mitigation: Repository-local Node scripts validated the same task and handoff files successfully; QA should continue using the repository script paths unless the global aliases are restored.

risks.summary: Replacing the rejected terrain drawing/spatial-index direction will touch schema/query/save, content/runtime, renderer/UI, AI/player explanations, and validation evidence across later tasks.
risks.mitigation: Keep the proposed task split and reviewer gates; do not collapse schema, fixture, renderer, and validation into one implementation task.

risks.summary: Terrain, port, river, pass, route, polity, label, or cultural-symbol claims can become false historical assertions if COMPOSITE fixture data is presented as verified history.
risks.mitigation: Fixture task routes real-world claims to historical_researcher and requires HISTORICAL/INFERRED/COMPOSITE/FICTIONAL tagging before formal content use.

risks.summary: The validation bar includes screenshot judgment that the map no longer reads as a disguised cell board.
risks.mitigation: Pair screenshots with concrete hit-test/query/route-preview/AI-reason parity tests and require QA review before product-owner reconsideration reopens.

requested_action: qa_reviewer should perform independent read-only review of ADR-011 and the DRAFT downstream task chain. Treat this as systems acceptance of the recovery planning package only: do not approve M7 content lock, do not start M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-SCHEMA-001 or any downstream implementation, and do not start M7-EXIT-VALIDATION-001.
